featuresSection.title: Everything you need for safe diving

howItWorks.title: AI dive intelligence,
howItWorks.subtitle: Tap a location, our AI runs a complete ocean-intelligence analysis, and returns a clear safety assessment. Here's what's happening behind the scenes.

pipeline.title: From a single tap to a full dive assessment
pipeline.subtitle: Our AI works through the analysis in clear, sequential stages. Each one is independently resilient — if any part has a hiccup, the rest continues.

locate.title: Locate the dive site
locate.description: Our AI identifies your dive site and prepares the analysis for that exact place.

bathymetry.title: Map seafloor & depth
bathymetry.description: We assess the underwater landscape — depth and terrain type for safe dive planning.

weather.title: Pull atmospheric weather
weather.description: Live weather conditions above the surface, for entry, exit and lightning safety.

marine.title: Pull marine forecast
marine.description: Live sea-state intelligence: surface motion, currents and water temperature.

tides.title: Calculate tides & slack
tides.description: We find the calmest moment in the tidal cycle — the optimal window to dive.

satellite.title: Read water clarity from satellite
satellite.description: AI analysis of live satellite imagery to understand how clear the water will be.

visibility.title: Compute visibility & thermocline
visibility.description: We estimate what you'll actually see underwater and where you'll feel a temperature change.

dynamics.title: Analyse surge & currents
dynamics.description: Our AI assesses in-water motion — how much divers will be moved around at depth.

safety.title: AI safety assessment
safety.description: The engine weighs every risk factor and applies built-in overrides for genuinely dangerous conditions.

ai.title: Generate recommendations
ai.description: Personalised wetsuit, certification level, marine-life alerts and best dive time — built for your exact site.

latency.title: What to expect for response time
latency.subtitle: Our AI is honest about how long the analysis takes. Most requests are fast, some are slow, and we tell you which is which.

fast.title: Quick analysis
fast.body: Familiar regions where our AI can answer almost instantly.

typical.title: Typical request
typical.body: A fresh AI assessment for any populated coastline.

cold.title: Deep analysis
cold.body: Remote coordinates where live satellite imagery has to be analysed on demand.

resilience.title: Built for the messy real world
resilience.subtitle: Even the best ocean intelligence has gaps. Our AI handles it.
resilience.body1: Every stage of the analysis is independently wrapped so that a transient hiccup cannot kill the entire request. When a signal degrades, our AI falls back to estimated values and clearly labels the affected fields.
resilience.body2: Our engine refuses to inflate the safety score when essential signals are missing. If we can't give you an honest answer, we tell you — we don't make one up.

safetyScoring.title: Your dive site, scored by
safetyScoring.subtitle: Our AI condenses live ocean intelligence into a single 0–100 safety score you can act on — at a glance.

intro.title: From complex ocean conditions to a clear answer
intro.subtitle: Our AI engine reasons over the full picture of your dive site — surface, depth, motion, weather, and water clarity — and translates it into a number divers actually understand.

intelligence.title: Intelligent risk weighting
intelligence.body: Our AI weighs every relevant ocean signal for your specific dive site and combines them into a single, intuitive safety score.

realtime.title: Live, never cached
realtime.body: Every score is computed fresh against current ocean intelligence. There are no recycled answers — your dive plan deserves up-to-the-minute analysis.

bands.title: Risk bands
bands.subtitle: What each score band means in practical terms — these are the labels you'll see in the app.

low.label: Low risk
low.description: Excellent or good diving conditions. Standard buddy procedures apply.

moderate.label: Moderate risk
moderate.description: Generally safe with awareness. Some conditions warrant attention.

high.label: High risk
high.description: Diveable only by experienced divers with appropriate gear and local knowledge.

extreme.label: Not recommended
extreme.description: Conditions are unsuitable today. Our AI recommends postponing the dive.

overrides.title: AI safety overrides
overrides.subtitle: Some conditions are dangerous regardless of any other positive signals. Our AI knows when to say no.
overrides.cardTitle: When our AI overrides positive signals
overrides.body: Even when most of your dive site's signals look good, certain situations make diving genuinely unsafe — and our AI will always force the score to zero in those cases. You'll see a clear explanation of which condition triggered the override so you know exactly what's wrong, not just that something is.

disclaimer.title: The score is decision support, not a permit to dive
disclaimer.body: A high safety score reflects modeled environmental conditions but cannot account for your individual experience, your equipment, your buddy's skill level, recent local events, or how your body feels on the day. Always do your own assessment, defer to your dive plan, and never dive against your gut.

faq.title: Questions,
faq.subtitle: Everything we get asked about how iSea works, what its limits are, and what your data is used for.

whatIsIsea.a: iSea is an AI-powered dive planning platform. You tap a location on a map, our engine analyses the environmental conditions for that exact spot, and returns a complete safety assessment — including visibility, currents, tides, wetsuit recommendation, and a 0–100 safety score.

isItAi.a: Our pipeline is an AI engine that autonomously analyses live ocean intelligence and applies decades of established oceanographic science to produce a single safety recommendation. It's not a deep-learning black box — we deliberately use transparent heuristics so dive safety decisions are auditable and explainable.

whyFiveMinutes.q: Why can dive-conditions take up to 5 minutes?
whyFiveMinutes.a: Most requests are fast. The slow case is when our AI has to run a deep analysis for a remote location it hasn't seen recently — that can take a couple of minutes. We show you a live progress view so you can see exactly what's happening and how far along we are.

accuracy.a: iSea's safety score is a forecast based on remotely-sensed environmental inputs. It cannot capture microclimate effects, time-of-day variability, or recent local events. Treat it as decision support, not a guarantee — always do your own assessment on the day.

offlineUse.q: Can I use iSea offline or underwater?
offlineUse.a: No. iSea requires an internet connection because every score is computed live from fresh upstream data. There's no cached score — that would be misleading.

replaceInstructor.q: Does iSea replace a dive instructor or local guide?
replaceInstructor.a: Absolutely not. iSea is decision support for already-certified divers. It cannot teach you to dive, cannot substitute for local knowledge, and cannot judge your personal readiness on a given day. Always defer to certified instructors and local operators for sites you don't know.

safetyScore.q: What does the safety score actually mean?
safetyScore.a: It's a 0–100 number that captures how safe diving conditions are at your exact site, right now. Our AI weighs all the relevant ocean factors and lands on a single score with a clear risk band (Low, Moderate, High, or Not Recommended). When something is genuinely dangerous, our AI overrides everything else and the score drops to zero with an explanation.

coldOpen.q: Why does the first request for a remote location take longer?
coldOpen.a: Our AI does a deeper analysis the first time it sees an unfamiliar region. Once a location has been analysed recently, subsequent requests for the same area are typically much faster.

lakes.q: Does iSea work for lakes and freshwater?
lakes.a: Partially. Our AI is primarily tuned for ocean and sea diving. Lake conditions can still be analysed but the safety score and visibility estimates will be less accurate than for marine sites. Dedicated freshwater intelligence is on our roadmap.

cost.a: Yes, iSea is free to use. Some upstream services have per-call costs that we absorb; we may introduce paid tiers in the future for high-volume professional use.

data.q: What data do you collect about me?
data.a: Account info (email, name), the dive locations you query, and your saved sites. We don't sell data and we don't track you across other sites. See the privacy policy for details.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.subtitle: The agreement between you and iSea when you use this service.
terms.summary: TL;DR — iSea is decision support, not a substitute for dive training or judgement on the day. Always read the <disclaimerLink>full safety disclaimer</disclaimerLink>. If anything in these terms is unclear, <contactLink>get in touch</contactLink>.

acceptance.title: Acceptance of terms
acceptance.body: By accessing or using iSea (the "Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree, do not use the Service.

eligibility.title: Eligibility
eligibility.body: You must be at least 16 years old to use iSea. By using the Service you represent that you meet this requirement and that you are using iSea in conjunction with appropriate dive training and certification.

account.title: Account responsibilities
account.body: You are responsible for safeguarding your account credentials and for any activity under your account. Notify us immediately of any unauthorized access.

license.title: License to use the Service
license.body: We grant you a limited, non-exclusive, non-transferable, revocable license to access and use the Service for your personal, non-commercial dive planning purposes.

restrictions.title: Acceptable use
restrictions.body: You will not: (a) reverse-engineer the Service; (b) use it to make decisions for others without their informed consent; (c) scrape, mirror, or redistribute our data; (d) misrepresent the Service's outputs as guarantees of safety; (e) use it for any unlawful purpose.

userContent.title: User-generated content
userContent.body: You retain ownership of dive sites, reviews, and notes you submit. You grant iSea a non-exclusive license to host, display and distribute that content within the Service. We may remove content that violates these Terms.

thirdParty.title: Third-party data
thirdParty.body: The Service incorporates data from third-party open and commercial oceanographic providers. Each provider's terms and licenses apply to their data; iSea does not warrant the accuracy of any third-party input. A list of providers and required attributions can be obtained on request.

disclaimerOfWarranties.title: Disclaimer of warranties
disclaimerOfWarranties.body: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE, OR NON-INFRINGEMENT. WE DO NOT WARRANT THAT SAFETY ASSESSMENTS WILL BE ACCURATE, COMPLETE, OR SUITABLE FOR ANY SPECIFIC DIVE.

limitationOfLiability.title: Limitation of liability
limitationOfLiability.body: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, ISEA AND ITS OPERATORS SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES, INCLUDING WITHOUT LIMITATION PERSONAL INJURY, DEATH, LOSS OF PROFITS, DATA OR USE, ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO YOUR USE OF OR INABILITY TO USE THE SERVICE. DIVING IS AN INHERENTLY HAZARDOUS ACTIVITY AND YOU ASSUME ALL RISK.

indemnification.title: Indemnification
indemnification.body: You agree to indemnify and hold harmless iSea and its operators from any claims, damages, losses, liabilities and expenses arising from your use of the Service or your violation of these Terms.

termination.title: Termination
termination.body: We may suspend or terminate your account at any time for violation of these Terms. You may delete your account at any time from your profile settings.

governingLaw.title: Governing law
governingLaw.body: These Terms are governed by the laws of the jurisdiction in which iSea is operated, without regard to conflict-of-law provisions.

changes.title: Changes to the Terms
changes.body: We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via the Service. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about these Terms can be sent through the contact channels listed on our home page.

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.subtitle: What we collect, why we collect it, and what we never do with your data.
privacy.summary: Short version: we collect the minimum needed to run the Service, we never sell your data, and we don't track you across other websites. You can delete your account and your data at any time.

whatWeCollect.title: What we collect
whatWeCollect.body: Account information you provide (email, name, password hash). Location coordinates you query for dive conditions. Dive sites, reviews and notes you create. Standard technical logs (IP address, browser type) needed to operate the Service.

howWeUse.title: How we use your data
howWeUse.body: To provide the Service (computing dive conditions, displaying your saved sites). To improve the Service (aggregate, anonymized usage analytics). To communicate essential service messages (account, security, terms updates).

legalBasis.title: Legal basis (GDPR)
legalBasis.body: We process your data under (a) contract — necessary to deliver the Service you signed up for; (b) legitimate interests — operational logging and abuse prevention; (c) consent — where required by law for analytics or marketing.

sharing.title: Who we share data with
sharing.body: We do not sell your personal data. We share data only with the infrastructure providers needed to operate the Service (hosting, database, email). Your dive site reviews are publicly visible by design.

thirdPartyApis.title: Third-party services we call on your behalf
thirdPartyApis.body: When you request dive conditions, your coordinates are sent to third-party oceanographic and meteorological data providers used by our pipeline. These services have their own privacy policies. We do not transmit your personal identity (account, name, email) to any of them.

cookies.title: Cookies and similar technologies
cookies.body: We use a small number of strictly necessary cookies for authentication and session management. We do not use third-party advertising cookies.

retention.title: Data retention
retention.body: Account data is retained as long as your account is active. Dive-conditions request logs are retained for a maximum of 12 months for operational debugging. Deleted accounts are purged within 30 days.

security.title: Security
security.body: Passwords are stored as one-way hashes. All traffic uses HTTPS. We follow industry-standard practices for protecting your data, but no system is 100% secure.

yourRights.title: Your rights
yourRights.body: You have the right to access, correct, export, and delete your personal data at any time. Most actions can be performed directly from your profile settings; for others, contact us.

children.title: Children's privacy
children.body: iSea is not directed at children under 16 and we do not knowingly collect data from them. If you believe a child has provided us data, contact us immediately.

transfers.title: International data transfers
transfers.body: Your data may be processed in countries other than your own. Where required, we rely on appropriate transfer mechanisms recognised under your jurisdiction's data-protection law.

changes.title: Changes to this policy
changes.body: We may update this policy from time to time. Material changes will be communicated via the Service.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Privacy questions and data-rights requests can be submitted through the contact channels listed on our home page.

disclaimer.title: Diving safety
disclaimer.subtitle: iSea is decision support, not a substitute for training, judgement, or local knowledge. Please read this carefully before using any safety assessment in your dive planning.

bigWarning.headline: Scuba diving is an inherently hazardous activity.
bigWarning.body: It carries risk of serious injury or death. No software product — including iSea — can eliminate that risk. The safety scores, recommendations, and warnings provided by iSea are informed estimates based on remotely-sensed environmental data and do not constitute a guarantee of safe diving conditions.

noSubstitute.title: Not a substitute for certified training
noSubstitute.body: iSea is intended for use by divers who hold a current certification from a recognised training agency (PADI, SSI, NAUI, BSAC, CMAS, etc.). It cannot teach you to dive, evaluate your personal readiness, or replace formal dive education.

estimates.title: All outputs are estimates
estimates.body: Visibility, currents, temperature, and other parameters are modeled or forecasted from third-party data sources. Real conditions can differ significantly due to local microclimates, recent weather events, or measurement gaps. Always verify on the day.

localConditions.title: Local knowledge is irreplaceable
localConditions.body: Currents, hazards, entry/exit logistics, and marine life behaviour at a specific site are best known to local operators and experienced divers familiar with the area. Where possible, dive with or consult someone who knows the site.

medical.title: Medical fitness is your responsibility
medical.body: iSea does not assess your medical fitness to dive. Recent illness, fatigue, alcohol, certain medications, ear/sinus conditions, and other medical factors can dramatically affect safety. If in doubt, consult a dive-medicine physician.

emergency.title: Always have an emergency plan
emergency.body: Know your nearest hyperbaric chamber, have surface support, file a dive plan with someone on shore, carry appropriate emergency signalling equipment (SMB, audible signal, mirror), and follow your training agency's emergency procedures.

disclaimer.acknowledgement: By using iSea you acknowledge that you have read and understood this disclaimer, that you accept all risk associated with diving, and that you will exercise your own judgement on every dive.

certification.label: Certification
certification.warnFallback: Your certification may be below what these conditions require.

depth.label: Planned depth
depth.pass: Within your preferred maximum of {{max}} m.
depth.warnFallback: Planned depth exceeds your preferred maximum.

suit.label: Exposure suit
suit.pass: Your usual suit is warm enough for these conditions.
suit.warnFallback: You may need a thicker suit than usual for this dive.

emergencyContact.label: Emergency contact
emergencyContact.warnFallback: No emergency contact on file. Add one in your profile.
